steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: B. pumilio's reference specimen is a holotype — the original specimen used for the first formal description. Sentence 2: G. inflata's reference specimen is a neotype — a replacement, not the original. The two specimens differ in kind.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: the two species have different types of reference specimens.
  • Choice A — "By contrast,": directly signals that the second case differs from the first. Fits: neotype vs. holotype is a meaningful distinction. ✓
  • Choice B — "Moreover,": signals addition of an equal-weight point, implying similarity or parity. The neotype/holotype difference is a contrast, not a parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — "Admittedly,": signals a concession — acknowledging something that weakens a prior claim. No claim is being conceded here. ✗
  • Choice D — "For instance,": signals a specific example of a general claim. The second specimen is not an example of the first — they are two separate cases being compared. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
